flashcards.front: What is the first step in IoT Design Methodology?
flashcards.back: Requirements Gathering and Analysis - Identifying the problem, defining objectives, understanding constraints, and determining functional/non-functional requirements for the IoT system.

flashcards.front: Name the five fundamental layers in IoT system architecture.
flashcards.back: 1) Perception Layer (Sensors/Actuators) 2) Network Layer (Communication) 3) Middleware Layer (Data Processing) 4) Application Layer (User Interface) 5) Business Layer (Analytics/Business Logic)

flashcards.front: What is the difference between functional and non-functional requirements in IoT?
flashcards.back: Functional requirements define what the system MUST do (data collection, transmission, actuation). Non-functional requirements define HOW well it must perform (scalability, latency, reliability, security, power consumption).

flashcards.front: What is prototyping in IoT design and why is it important?
flashcards.back: Prototyping is creating a preliminary version of the IoT system to validate design choices, test sensor integration, verify communication protocols, and identify issues before full-scale development, reducing development cost and time.

flashcards.front: What factors should be considered when selecting hardware for an IoT device?
flashcards.back: Processing capability, power consumption, memory, connectivity options (WiFi/BLE/Zigbee), sensor compatibility, form factor, cost, and operating conditions (temperature, humidity).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between MQTT and CoAP protocols in IoT?
flashcards.back: MQTT is publish-subscribe based, connection-oriented (TCP), suitable for bandwidth-constrained scenarios. CoAP is request-response based, uses UDP, designed for resource-constrained devices with RESTful architecture.

flashcards.front: What is edge computing in IoT design context?
flashcards.back: Edge computing involves processing data near the source (at the edge of the network) rather than sending all data to the cloud. This reduces latency, bandwidth usage, and improves response time for time-critical applications.

flashcards.front: List the key security considerations in IoT design methodology.
flashcards.back: 1) Device authentication 2) Data encryption (in transit and at rest) 3) Secure boot 4) Firmware updates 5) Access control 6) Network segmentation 7) Privacy protection 8) Vulnerability management

flashcards.front: What is data management strategy in IoT systems?
flashcards.back: It involves deciding what data to collect, storage location (edge/cloud/hybrid), data formats, retention policies, preprocessing needs, and analytics approach to derive meaningful insights from raw sensor data.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of logical design in IoT?
flashcards.back: Logical design creates a technology-agnostic representation of the system, defining data flows, functional modules, interfaces between components, and system behavior without committing to specific hardware or software implementations.

flashcards.front: What is vertical integration in IoT system design?
flashcards.back: Vertical integration refers to the end-to-end integration of all system components from sensors to cloud platform, ensuring seamless data flow and interoperability across all layers of the IoT architecture.

flashcards.front: What are the phases of IoT system development lifecycle?
flashcards.back: 1) Requirement Analysis 2) System Design 3) Implementation 4) Testing 5) Deployment 6) Maintenance 7) Iteration/Upgradation - following iterative rather than strictly linear progression.
